commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6041ae88394e01aee5531d815cc4c3652b5071bf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR routes Feishu p2p top-level replies through a sender `user:<open_id>` delivery target, updates direct send/media/card paths, and adds a non-streaming p2p regression test.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +32. Total +38 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: p2p dispatch passes `deliveryTarget: user:<open_id>` and `skipReplyToInMessages: true`, but PR-head streaming start still receives `replyToMessageId`. `FeishuStreamingSession` chooses `im.message.reply` whenever that option exists.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed because the code blocker is narrow, but the external PR still lacks real Feishu p2p proof that automation cannot supply.

target.title: fix(feishu): send p2p replies to user target
target.body:
## Summary
- route Feishu p2p top-level replies through the sender `user:<open_id>` target instead of the p2p `oc_*` chat id
- keep typing and reply-to lifecycle tied to the inbound message while suppressing reply metadata for DM sends
- add regression coverage for p2p direct reply target selection and direct silent-reply context

Fixes #83730

## Tests
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: Feishu p2p replies now send to the `user:<open_id>` delivery target instead of the p2p `oc_*` chat id, while reply metadata remains tied to the inbound message lifecycle.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `6041ae88392724dd3fe1288b8d97a1e17ebd60e3` on macOS 15.5 with Node v22.22.1 and pnpm 11.2.2; no live Feishu credentials were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts`; `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the PR-head checkout:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts
✓  extension-feishu  extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts (78 tests) 40ms
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 14.49s

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts
✓  extension-feishu  extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts (73 tests) 76ms
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 9.35s

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts
✓  extension-feishu  extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts (61 tests) 18ms
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 5.99s

$ git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
# no output; exit code 0
```

- Observed result after fix: all Feishu reply-dispatcher, bot, and channel shards passed at PR head, covering p2p `user:<open_id>` direct reply target selection and silent-reply context; the PR diff has no whitespace errors.
- What was not tested: live Feishu p2p delivery against a real tenant; this proof uses the local Feishu plugin runtime harness with synthetic message ids and redacted-style targets.
target.author: leno23
